evidence_quotes.quote: when the developer decies to shut the switch down, the game becomes unusable.
Games are advertised as products but threated as services.

paragraphs.text: I'm proud to be an European citizen and I hope this act can change the gaming industry and the digital part of our world (or at least in Europe) in general. Every year a lot of games (and probably software as well) are being killed. Also online products destroyed. You can easily loose the games you paid for. Everything requires some sort of a server so it can connects to it and when the developer decies to shut the switch down, the game becomes unusable. Or revoke your access to the product you paid for at any time. What's the difference between buying a movie, a book or a video game? Imagine when you buy a book and you can have it forever... There's no need for the author to support the book but you can still read it at any time. Well this should be possible with the games too. Developers must have an 'end of life' plan where they make the game playable offline or allow other passionate groups/community members to run the required server/s for the game. Also a lot of developers use more and more all sorts of tricks/shenanigans and other methods to earn profits by selling lootboxes/currencies for real money without any proper regulations and this is unhealthy, especially for the younger audience and the children. And when the game is killed you loose all of your purchases. We should have coverage so you can access the things you've paid for. Games are advertised as products but threated as services. A proper customer protection is necessary. This is our right!
